questions.question: Solve |x| = 7
questions.explanation: Absolute value equals 7 when x is either 7 units to the right or 7 units to the left of 0 on the number line.

questions.question: Solve |x - 2| = 5
questions.explanation: The expression (x - 2) equals 5 or (x - 2) equals -5. So x = 7 or x = -3.

questions.question: Solve |2x| = 12
questions.explanation: Divide both sides by 2: |x| = 6. So x = 6 or x = -6.

questions.question: Which values of x satisfy |x + 3| = 1?
questions.explanation: Set x + 3 = 1 to get x = -2, and x + 3 = -1 to get x = -4.

questions.question: A number's distance from 0 is 8. Write an absolute value equation and solve it.
questions.explanation: Distance from 0 means absolute value. The equation |x| = 8 gives two solutions: 8 and -8.

questions.question: Solve |x| = -3
questions.explanation: Absolute value is always non-negative, so it can never equal -3. The equation has no solution.
